text.title: Product-Led Growth (PLG) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Activation rate <15%; first-session funnel exits before aha event.
antipattern.root-cause: Onboarding flow takes 9+ minutes to first value.
antipattern.fix: Re-design onboarding for aha < 5 min; cut steps, defer profile completion.

antipattern.symptom: Sales gets PQL handoffs that haven't logged in for 3 weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: PQL score uses email-engagement signals; no usage signals.
antipattern.fix: templates/pql-scorer.py MUST score on real usage (workspaces, integrations, invited collaborators, depth events).

antipattern.symptom: Self-serve conversion stuck; users hit 'book a call' wall to upgrade.
antipattern.root-cause: Pricing page gates paid tier behind sales call.
antipattern.fix: Remove the sales-required gate; offer sales-assist as opt-in path for enterprise.

antipattern.symptom: Upgrade emails sent on day 7 / 14 / 21 regardless of usage; reply: 'unsubscribe'.
antipattern.root-cause: Triggers based on calendar, not on hitting usage limits.
antipattern.fix: Fire upgrade prompts on limit-hit events only; deprecate calendar campaigns for PLG accounts.
